_comment: Q&A page generation queue. Each entry produces ONE JSON file under content/data/qa/<slug>.json via the coveredusa-qa-writer agent. Status: 'queued' / 'writing' / 'verified' / 'flagged' / 'write_failed'.

qa.question: Does Medicare Cover Vision?
qa.notes: Sister page to does-medicare-cover-dental. Original Medicare excludes routine vision, MA often includes limited vision benefit. Cataract surgery is the main exception (covered under Part B as medically necessary).

qa.question: Does Medicare Cover Hearing Aids?
qa.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover hearing aids. MA plans may include limited hearing benefit. Hearing exam covered only if doctor-ordered for medical diagnosis. Cite KFF analysis.

qa.question: Does Medicaid Cover Dental? (State-by-State 2026)
qa.notes: Dental is OPTIONAL Medicaid benefit (states choose). Most states cover at least emergency. Coverage breakdown: 5 categories — comprehensive, limited, emergency-only, no adult coverage. Reference Medicaid.gov state benefit tracker.

qa.question: Does the ACA Cover Pre-existing Conditions?
qa.notes: YES — ACA prohibits denying coverage or charging more for pre-existing conditions. This is permanent law per ACA 2010. Still relevant because some short-term limited duration insurance (STLDI) plans CAN exclude pre-existing — important caveat.

qa.question: Does Medicaid Cover Drug Rehab?
qa.notes: YES — Medicaid covers substance use treatment in all 50 states (ACA EHB requirement). Coverage includes inpatient, outpatient, MAT (methadone, buprenorphine). State variation on details. Reference SAMHSA + Medicaid.gov.

qa.question: Does Medicare Cover Cataract Surgery?
qa.notes: YES — covered under Part B. Patient pays 20% after Part B deductible ($283 in 2026). Lens upgrade (premium IOL) NOT covered, patient pays difference (~$1,500-$4,000 per eye). Common Medicare beneficiary surgery. ctaTarget=analyzer because the answer discusses out-of-pocket cost percentages.

qa.question: Can I Get Medicaid If I Work?
qa.notes: YES, if income below state Medicaid threshold (138% FPL in expansion states). Working alone does not disqualify. Reference 2026 FPL: $15,960 hh-1, 138% = $22,025. State-by-state variation.

qa.question: Is the ACA Marketplace the Same as Obamacare?
qa.notes: YES — same thing. 'Obamacare' is the colloquial name for the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (ACA) of 2010. Marketplace = the exchange where ACA plans are sold. Brief terminology Q&A. pageType=terminology means OMIT coverageBreakdown.

qa.question: What Counts as Income for Medicaid?
qa.notes: MAGI (Modified Adjusted Gross Income) for most categories. Includes wages, self-employment, taxable Social Security, capital gains. EXCLUDES: child support, SSI, some VA benefits. Reference IRS + healthcare.gov MAGI definition. pageType=definition means OMIT coverageBreakdown.

qa.question: Does Medicare Cover Physical Therapy?
qa.notes: YES — covered under Part B. Patient pays 20% after $283 Part B deductible (2026). No annual cap since 2018 KX modifier rules. Doctor must certify medical necessity. MA plans may have prior auth + cap. ctaTarget=analyzer because the answer is dominated by cost-share discussion.
